name: Kukkakaalialaatikko (Finnish Cualiflower Rye Casserole)
recipeIngredient: 3 cups rye bread cubes (darker the better)
1 head cauliflower, cut into bite-sized florets
4 egg equivalents (dry replacer in 1/2 cup water, 8 whites, etc.)
1 tsp dry mustard (or 2 tsp Dijon style prepared)
freshly ground black pepper to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Pour the beer into a shallow bowl and let it sit until flat.
2. Dry bread cubes on a baking sheet at 300°F (150°C) for about 15 minutes in an oven.
3. Lightly coat a 2-quart casserole dish with non-stick spray.
4. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C).
5. In a large non-stick skillet, steam/saute cauliflower with caraway seeds and 2 tbsp water until barely tender.
6. Combine bread cubes and cauliflower in the prepared casserole dish.
7. In a large bowl, whisk together egg equivalents, nutritional yeast, mustard, coriander, pepper, and flat beer.
8. Pour the liquid over the bread and cauliflower in the casserole dish.
9. Bake for 30 to 45 minutes until puffed and golden.
